title: The One Where Joey Moves Out

jokes.text: Ever realize Cap'n Crunch's eyebrows are on his hat?
jokes.setup: Character makes random cereal observation
jokes.punchline: Absurd detail about cartoon mascot anatomy
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about cartoon anatomy, economical delivery

jokes.text: That's what's weird? The man's been captain of a cereal for 40 years.
jokes.setup: Response to eyebrow observation
jokes.punchline: Points out the truly absurd thing - being a cereal captain for decades
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that reframes the absurdity, great economy

jokes.text: The spoon! You licked and you put.
jokes.setup: Character grossed out by roommate's behavior
jokes.punchline: Broken grammar from disgust - 'licked and you put'
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler neurosis, memorable phrasing that became quotable

jokes.text: Only because I used the red one to unclog the drain.
jokes.setup: Accused of using roommate's toothbrush
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of even grosser behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - casual about disgusting behavior, great escalation

jokes.text: Mine is the red one! Oh, God! Can open. Worms everywhere!
jokes.setup: Realizes Joey used his toothbrush for drain
jokes.punchline: Metaphor for escalating gross revelations
jokes.explanation: Excellent metaphor, perfectly timed revelation, classic Chandler delivery

jokes.text: Because soap is soap. It's self-cleaning.
jokes.setup: Why can't they share toothbrush but can share soap
jokes.punchline: Joey's confident but nonsensical logic
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey pseudo-wisdom delivered with confidence

jokes.text: The next time you shower...think about the last thing I wash and the first thing you wash.
jokes.setup: Continuing soap sharing argument
jokes.punchline: Implied anatomy joke about soap usage order
jokes.explanation: Clever innuendo that makes the point without being explicit

jokes.text: Look at you fancy lads!
jokes.setup: Seeing friends dressed up
jokes.punchline: Joey's attempt at sophisticated vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Minor character moment, Joey trying to sound fancy

jokes.text: He's having a brunch.
jokes.setup: Guy on show in coma
jokes.punchline: Coma patient hosting brunch
jokes.explanation: Absurd soap opera logic delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: That's lucky. What if her name was Big Ugly Splotch?
jokes.setup: Getting lily tattoo because mom's name is Lily
jokes.punchline: Absurd hypothetical alternative name
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation with visual irony of tattoo result

jokes.text: A chip.
jokes.setup: Ross asks what's on shoulder, expecting tattoo answer
jokes.punchline: Monica deflects with 'chip on shoulder' idiom
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay deflection, very economical

jokes.text: It's like having a bad haircut all the time.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining why tattoos are bad
jokes.punchline: Analogy to permanent bad haircut
jokes.explanation: Solid analogy that captures Ross's neurotic perspective

jokes.text: Yeah, for my dad's birthday, I decided to give him a stroke.
jokes.setup: Asked if she'll tell parents about Richard today
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic dark humor about dad's potential reaction
jokes.explanation: Dark sarcasm that perfectly captures Monica's anxiety about the situation

jokes.text: Remember when you were 9 and Richard was 30, Dad said: 'God, I hope they get together.'
jokes.setup: Ross sarcastically reassuring Monica about parents' reaction
jokes.punchline: Highlighting the creepy age gap with fake parental approval
jokes.explanation: Sharp sarcasm that exposes the uncomfortable reality of the age gap

jokes.text: You could come in straddling him, they still wouldn't believe it.
jokes.setup: Monica worried about looking like they're hiding something
jokes.punchline: Absurd escalation of how oblivious parents are
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke about parental obliviousness

jokes.text: Actually, Mom, I think Monica thanked him for the both of us.
jokes.setup: Mother asks if they thanked Dr. Burke for ride
jokes.punchline: Double entendre about Monica 'thanking' Richard sexually
jokes.explanation: Subtle innuendo that works on two levels

jokes.text: Finger cramp! Oh, God! Sorry. Here, let me get that, Mom.
jokes.setup: Monica hearing parents talk about Richard's 'twinkie'
jokes.punchline: Physical reaction covers her emotional distress
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy covering emotional discomfort

jokes.text: You just know she's got the IQ of a napkin.
jokes.setup: Talking about Richard's young girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Comparing intelligence to absorbent paper product
jokes.explanation: Catty insult with memorable comparison

jokes.text: Just young enough so that everything is still pointing up.
jokes.setup: Continuing description of young girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic reference to youthful body
jokes.explanation: Clever euphemism delivered by mother character adds extra humor

jokes.text: When you're in there, it's like peeing with the Rockettes.
jokes.setup: Describing mirrors on both sides in bathroom
jokes.punchline: Absurd comparison to famous dance troupe
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd comparison that's both visual and perfectly Joey

jokes.text: Well, there's my fantasy come true.
jokes.setup: Joey's Rockettes bathroom comment
jokes.punchline: Dry, sarcastic reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler deadpan response

jokes.text: That's all right, I saw a kitchen this morning. On TV.
jokes.setup: Offered to see the kitchen
jokes.punchline: Joey's logic that TV kitchen viewing counts
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey logic delivered with confidence

jokes.text: Stop talking. Okay!
jokes.setup: Joey's TV kitchen comment
jokes.punchline: Chandler's exasperated attempt to control Joey
jokes.explanation: Minor character reaction beat

jokes.text: You got your own little speedster.
jokes.setup: Dad comparing Richard's situation to his midlife crisis Porsche
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic reference to young girlfriend as sports car
jokes.explanation: Dad's attempt at being cool with car metaphor

jokes.text: Dad, I beg you not to finish that sentence!
jokes.setup: Dad about to suggest borrowing Richard's 'speedster'
jokes.punchline: Monica's horror at the implication
jokes.explanation: Monica's mortification at dad's innuendo

jokes.text: You know I'd never let him touch the Porsche.
jokes.setup: Seeming to back down from inappropriate suggestion
jokes.punchline: Actually just protective of his car, not being appropriate
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - he's not being appropriate, just selfish

jokes.text: Foghorn Leghorn!
jokes.setup: Phoebe describing her mother's open spirit
jokes.punchline: Random cartoon rooster exclamation
jokes.explanation: Pure Phoebe randomness, completely unexpected

jokes.text: What's 1922? Just, you know, a long time ago. When men used to tell women what to do a lot.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to make historical reference
jokes.punchline: Vague, imprecise historical knowledge
jokes.explanation: Rachel's ditzy attempt at being historically informed

jokes.text: Then there was suffrage, which was a good thing. But it sounds horrible.
jokes.setup: Rachel continuing historical explanation
jokes.punchline: Confusing word sound with meaning
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay on 'suffrage' sounding like 'suffering'

jokes.text: You march in there and get that tattoo on your hip!
jokes.setup: Empowering Rachel about her autonomy
jokes.punchline: Gets body part wrong
jokes.explanation: Minor error comedy from minor character

jokes.text: How you doing? I'm a twinkie! Really? I'm a hero.
jokes.setup: Monica and Richard meeting up secretly
jokes.punchline: Playing with their 'types' - dessert vs sandwich
jokes.explanation: Cute wordplay on food items

jokes.text: My parents are dead. God, you are so lucky! I mean... Well, you know what I mean.
jokes.setup: Monica suggests telling Richard's parents first
jokes.punchline: Dark humor about dead parents being convenient
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny social awkwardness, realistic uncomfortable moment

jokes.text: Judy! Going to the bathroom. Good for you!
jokes.setup: Richard trying to act normal around Monica's parents
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly over-enthusiastic about mundane activity
jokes.explanation: Awkward overcompensation comedy

jokes.text: I know. He's a new man. It's like a scene from Cocoon.
jokes.setup: Discussing Richard's rejuvenation
jokes.punchline: Reference to movie about elderly people becoming young
jokes.explanation: Dated but appropriate pop culture reference

jokes.text: With you, it's like I've got two 25-year-olds.
jokes.setup: Wife asks about trading her for younger model
jokes.punchline: Dad's attempt at complimenting wife's... assets
jokes.explanation: Dad's crude but affectionate compliment

jokes.text: I saw the way you were checking out his moldings.
jokes.setup: Chandler denying interest in apartment
jokes.punchline: Architectural feature made to sound sexual
jokes.explanation: Subtle innuendo using home improvement terminology

jokes.text: It wouldn't kill you to say it once in a while.
jokes.setup: Joey talking about their apartment like it's his girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Needy, relationship-like demand for affirmation
jokes.explanation: Joey treating apartment like romantic relationship

jokes.text: I may have to bring in somebody once a week to lick the silverware.
jokes.setup: Joey saying he can afford to live alone
jokes.punchline: Callback to earlier hygiene argument with absurd solution
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that escalates the original hygiene joke absurdly

jokes.text: We're not Bert and Ernie.
jokes.setup: Chandler pointing out they didn't commit to live together forever
jokes.punchline: Sesame Street reference to famous roommate duo
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference to illustrate point

jokes.text: You can spend more quality time with your real friends...the spoons!
jokes.setup: Joey being hurt about Chandler wanting to move
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic callback to spoon hygiene fight
jokes.explanation: Good emotional callback using earlier comedy beat

jokes.text: Well, I just caught the live show.
jokes.setup: Ross mentioning parents' old video
jokes.punchline: Monica saw parents being intimate in real time
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff with relatable awkwardness

jokes.text: No, a doctor of meat. Of course he's a real doctor.
jokes.setup: Mother asks if he's a real doctor
jokes.punchline: Monica's sarcastic response about fake doctorate
jokes.explanation: Monica's defensive sarcasm about Richard's credentials

jokes.text: I'm the twinkie.
jokes.setup: Dad says Richard has twinkie, that's impossible
jokes.punchline: Monica reveals she IS the twinkie
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic reveal with great economy, pivotal moment

jokes.text: Upstairs in the bathroom, right before you felt up Mom!
jokes.setup: Dad asks when he said Richard was happy
jokes.punchline: Monica reveals she witnessed parents' intimate moment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and callback to earlier bathroom incident

jokes.text: And did you know they do this with needles?
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining why she didn't get tattoo
jokes.punchline: Shocked discovery of obvious fact about tattoo process
jokes.explanation: Rachel's naive shock at obvious reality

jokes.text: Really? You don't say? Because mine was licked on by kittens!
jokes.setup: Rachel's obvious statement about needles
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's sarcastic escalation with absurd alternative
jokes.explanation: Brilliant sarcastic response with absurd imagery, perfect Phoebe delivery

jokes.text: Lick away, my man!
jokes.setup: Chandler giving Joey plastic spoons as peace offering
jokes.punchline: Encouraging the behavior he was previously disgusted by
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing relationship reconciliation through earlier conflict

jokes.text: I've never been lower or wetter.
jokes.setup: Joey asking if Chandler's okay with him leaving
jokes.punchline: Chandler twists 'high and dry' idiom for emphasis
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay inversion showing Chandler's emotional state

jokes.text: Your men will get scored on more times than your sister.
jokes.setup: Joey trash talking before foosball game
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo about Chandler's sister
jokes.explanation: Crude sports trash talk with sexual innuendo

jokes.text: Which sister?
jokes.setup: Joey's insult about his sister
jokes.punchline: Chandler's protective concern about which sister
jokes.explanation: Good protective brother reaction

jokes.text: This is a tattoo of the Earth as seen from a great, great distance. It's the way my mother sees me from heaven.
jokes.setup: Rachel defending her tiny dot tattoo
jokes.punchline: Elaborate explanation for obvious failure
jokes.explanation: Creative rationalization for failure, sweet sentiment made absurd

jokes.text: Your mother's in heaven going, 'Where the hell's my lily, you wuss!'
jokes.setup: Rachel's sweet explanation about Earth tattoo
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's harsh reality check from deceased mother
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of Rachel's sweet sentiment, perfect Phoebe bluntness

jokes.text: We used it to fling water balloons off the roof. Remember? Those kids couldn't even get theirs across the street.
jokes.setup: Monica finding her bra in Joey's stuff
jokes.punchline: Innocent explanation for having women's undergarment as weapon
jokes.explanation: Absurd but innocent explanation for compromising situation

jokes.text: You keep it. You need the practice.
jokes.setup: Chandler offers to help with foosball table
jokes.punchline: Joey's final dig about Chandler's foosball skills
jokes.explanation: Good final jab between friends, shows their dynamic

tmdb_overview: Joey moves out following a fight with Chandler. Rachel and Phoebe consider tattoos. Mr. Geller learns his best friend is dating his daughter.
